body.question_1: How would you rate the technical competency of the student intern in their relevant field of study?
body.answer_1: The intern's technical competency revealed substandard gaps between what the School of Business Administration's International Business curriculum covers and the practical skills required in a professional workplace environment. While the intern possessed a reasonable theoretical grounding in core concepts, they struggled to translate this knowledge into practical application without significant guidance and hand-holding from senior team members. Proficiency in industry-standard tools and workflows was below the level we typically expect from graduates at a comparable stage, and several foundational technical skills needed to be reinforced on the job. We strongly recommend the School of Business Administration review its curriculum to better align academic content with current industry tool and practice standards.

body.question_2: How well did the intern demonstrate professional communication and interpersonal skills in the workplace?
body.answer_2: The professional attitude demonstrated by this intern from the School of Business Administration's International Business program was commendable and genuinely impressed our team throughout the internship period. Punctuality, reliability, and a consistently positive and collaborative work ethic were evident from the very first week, requiring no correction or management intervention to maintain. The intern communicated proactively, raised concerns and questions through appropriate channels, and responded to feedback constructively and without defensiveness. Their professional maturity — particularly in managing competing deadlines, handling ambiguity, and engaging respectfully with colleagues at all levels — reflects a level of preparation that speaks well of the School of Business Administration's commitment to producing work-ready graduates.

body.question_3: How effectively did the intern collaborate with team members and contribute to group projects?
body.answer_3: The intern integrated into our team environment commendablely and became a valued collaborative contributor from an early stage of the internship, consistently demonstrating the interpersonal skills required to function effectively in a professional team setting. They participated actively in team meetings, contributed ideas respectfully and constructively, and took clear ownership of their assigned workstreams without requiring constant supervision or direction from colleagues. During group project phases, the intern's ability to coordinate tasks, manage shared deadlines, and maintain positive relationships under pressure was excellent and significantly contributed to successful outcomes. These teamwork capabilities reflect very well on the interpersonal skills development embedded in the School of Business Administration's International Business program.

body.question_4: How would you assess the intern's problem-solving abilities and approach to challenging or unfamiliar tasks?
body.answer_4: The intern's problem-solving abilities were among the most commendable attributes they brought to our organization from the School of Business Administration's International Business program. When confronted with unfamiliar challenges or technical obstacles, the intern approached them systematically — breaking problems into manageable components, forming and testing hypotheses, and escalating appropriately only when genuinely blocked by limitations beyond their current capability. Their intellectual curiosity, persistence under pressure, and willingness to explore unconventional solutions made them a genuinely valuable problem-solver within the team. This quality is difficult to teach in the workplace and speaks highly of the critical thinking culture embedded in the School of Business Administration's academic programs.

body.question_5: How quickly and effectively did the intern adapt to your company's work culture, tools, and environment?
body.answer_5: This intern showed decent adaptability to our work environment, adjusting reasonably well to most situations while requiring additional support when changes occurred at a faster pace or scale than anticipated. Day-to-day routine adjustments — such as modified task priorities or new tool adoption — were handled efficiently and without notable disruption. More significant changes, such as project pivots or team restructuring, required more guidance to navigate smoothly and confidently. Greater exposure to agile methodologies, sprint-based workflows, and change management principles in the International Business curriculum would strengthen the adaptability of graduates entering dynamic professional environments.

body.question_6: How proficient was the intern in English communication, both written and verbal, for professional purposes?
body.answer_6: English communication capability for this intern was acceptable, sufficient for routine internal tasks but requiring development to meet the standards expected in professional, client-facing, or international contexts. Reading comprehension of English-language materials was generally strong, enabling effective research and document processing independently. However, written composition — particularly formal reports and professional emails — required editing support, and verbal fluency in meetings was limited, particularly when discussions moved at pace or involved complex technical vocabulary. Strengthening English for Professional Purposes training in the International Business curriculum would meaningfully enhance graduates' communication readiness for the modern, increasingly international workplace.

body.question_7: How well did the intern's academic training align with the practical demands and standards of your industry?
body.answer_7: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Business Administration's International Business program, based on this internship experience, is decent: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_8: What specific technical or soft skill areas do you believe the university curriculum should strengthen for future graduates?
body.answer_8: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Business Administration's International Business program, based on this internship experience, is moderate: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_9: How would you rate the intern's overall performance and value contribution to your organization during the placement?
body.answer_9: This intern's overall performance was decent, delivering a useful contribution to the team while also clearly indicating areas where additional academic preparation would improve professional readiness. Assigned tasks were generally completed to a functional standard, with quality improving progressively as the intern built familiarity with our tools, processes, and expectations over the placement period. The growth trajectory was positive and suggests genuine potential that a stronger academic foundation would help realize more quickly. We see this internship as a successful learning experience for the student and encourage the School of Business Administration to continue building practical readiness into its curriculum to produce even more effective graduates.

body.question_10: Based on your experience with this intern, would you consider hiring from this university in the future, and why?
body.answer_10: While we respect the School of Business Administration as an institution and do not question its academic credentials, our experience with this particular intern from the International Business program has given us insufficient reservations about recruiting heavily from this cohort in the near future. The skill gaps and professional readiness challenges encountered during this placement represent a significant onboarding investment that strains our capacity as a host organization. We would encourage the School of Business Administration to engage directly with industry partners — including ourselves — to understand the specific competency gaps and develop targeted solutions before we could confidently increase our intake of graduates from this program. We remain open to future engagement if meaningful curriculum improvements are implemented and communicated to the industry community.
